---
layout: post
title: "The Inspector That Ran Your Slip"
subtitle: "HTB DevHub, where a debug console left facing the street runs whatever command you name, a blind box is made to hold its answer up to the glass, and every service hands you the exact secret that opens the next one"
date: 2026-07-11 12:00:00 +0000
description: "HTB DevHub walkthrough. A medium Linux box built as a ladder of MCP services, each running as the next user up and leaking the one secret you need: unauth inspector RCE, blind in-band exfil, ps-leaked token, root key dump. Sealed while the box is live."
image: /assets/og/devhub.png
tags: [hackthebox, writeup]
---

DevHub looks like a place that ships developer tooling, and that is exactly how it dies. The front page is a painted door with nothing behind it, and the real machine is a stack of MCP services wired nose to tail, each one running as the next user up the ladder and each one leaking the precise secret that opens the door above it. It starts with a debugging console left facing the internet with no password, a tool built to trust whoever holds it, now held by you. The box is blind and it will not phone home, so the whole middle of the game is making a silent machine hold its answers up to a window you were always allowed to look through. Nothing here is memory corruption. Every lock is somebody trusting a config field, a process list, or a hardcoded key to be exactly the harmless thing it claimed to be.

```
        d e v h u b            (medium · linux)
        =========================
        the front door is painted on.
        the real one has no lock at all.
                        |
                        v
        you name a command.   the inspector runs it.
        you ask what happened. the box says nothing.
                        |
                        v
        so you stop asking it to speak, and you make
        it hold the answer up to a window you can see.
                        |
                        v
        every service runs as the next user up, and
        leaks the one secret that opens the door above.
                                              . . .
```

The full chain, from the one open inspector to root's own key, is written up below in the usual detail. Because DevHub is **still active on Hack The Box**, that write-up is sealed. It unseals the moment the box retires. If you already hold the passphrase, the gate is right here.

<script type="application/json" id="lock-data">{"v":1,"iter":250000,"salt":"XXdfq4GaOhK2RCltT30Vxg==","iv":"NZvysjvMh0fKgLbX","ct":""}</script>

{% include locked-writeup.html %}

---

*HTB: DevHub, a medium Linux box that is really a lecture on debug tools left facing the street, secrets left where the wrong reader can see them, and why egress filtering is a delay and not a wall. Still active. The full chain unseals here the day it retires, no live payloads either way.*
